sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated detail-page parity is still pending.

Most genes and variants behind the one-page sample's nutrition table are not visible in the sample PDF and are not inferred.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis of lactose intolerance.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, ancestry, diet history, and non-genetic causes also matter.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report uses a table of nutrition-related traits with result labels such as Lower, Typical, Much Higher, Slower, Intolerant, Very Reduced, More Able, and PAV/AVI.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These are observed sample rows for report structure only. They are not local-user diagnoses, diet advice, supplement advice, food-safety claims, vitamin-status claims, lab interpretations, or treatment guidance.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Food Allergy with a Lower result, 1 in 20 lifetime-risk context, and an immune-reaction description.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal food-allergy diagnosis or food-safety conclusion.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Peanut Allergy with a Much Higher result, 1 in 100 lifetime-risk context, and 80% heritable text.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal peanut-allergy diagnosis, exposure guidance, or actionability statement.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Caffeine Metabolism with a Slower result and says caffeine metabolism is mainly determined by a cytochrome enzyme.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not personal caffeine-consumption, sleep, medication, or disease-risk advice.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Dietary Fat Weight Gain Response with a Lower result and shows Lower Gain, Typical, and Higher Gain comparison labels.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal weight-loss, diet-fat, meal-plan, or metabolic conclusion.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Lactose Intolerance with an Intolerant result and describes lactose as sugar in dairy products.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal lactose-intolerance diagnosis, symptom explanation, or diet prescription.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Beta-ionone floral perception with a Very Reduced result and describes beta-ionone as fragrant and floral in foods and beverages.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal food preference or nutrition recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Bitter Taste Perception with a More Able result and lists super-, medium-, and non-taster comparison labels.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal vegetable intake, diet, or health recommendation.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Vitamin B6 Level with a Lower result and notes a normal range for the biologically active form of Vitamin B6.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal vitamin level, deficiency, supplement, lab, or treatment conclusion.

sampleRows.description: The sample table labels Vitamin B9 Processing Efficiency with a Lower result and says folate is converted by the body from folic acid.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the Nutrition Related sample table; this is observed sample output structure, not a personal folate status, supplement, pregnancy, lab, or treatment conclusion.

sampleRows.description: The sample footer explains PTC bitter-taste variation and states that the sample TAS2R38 genotype is PAV/AVI.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the sample footer; this is observed sample output structure, not a local user's TAS2R38 result unless supplied by local validated evidence.

nutritionContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a diet, allergy, vitamin, lab, or supplement plan.
Track symptoms and dietary history with a clinician or registered dietitian if lactose tolerance, food reactions, or nutrition status are concerns.
Do not change foods, supplements, medication, or medical care based on genotype or sample-only rows alone.

references.title: Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

genotypeSummary.meaning: Local derived evidence is available for lactose-related context only.
This genotype appears in the sample report footer and is not a local-user result.
The sample table exposes result labels, but most gene and variant mappings are not visible and are not inferred.

missingInputs: Authenticated detail-page body parity is not yet captured.
No calibrated nutrition probability model is supplied.
No food-allergy diagnosis, vitamin lab value, symptom history, medication list, diet history, supplement context, or ancestry-applicability model is supplied.
Most sample-table genes and variants are not visible in the one-page sample PDF.
uncertainty: Nutrition traits, symptoms, allergy status, vitamin status, and food reactions can be influenced by genetics, ancestry, diet history, environment, labs, medical history, and other non-genetic factors.
A single marker or sample-only row should not be used to diagnose lactose intolerance, food allergy, nutrient deficiency, or prescribe a diet or supplement.
The sample rows are output-format evidence, not local-user findings.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence and sample-report structure only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not diagnose lactose intolerance, food allergy, vitamin deficiency, or nutrient deficiency.
The report does not prescribe diet changes, recommend supplements, infer lab values, or claim foods are safe or unsafe.
